Key Insight: The dataset contains a total of 11,362 MRI slices across 194 unique cases spanning 4 clinical classes. Prostate masks are the most abundant with 5,961 annotated slices.

Dataset Overview

The dataset is organized into 4 classes, representing different clinical categories. The distribution shows imbalanced data across classes, with the largest class containing 3,489 images and the smallest containing 1,024 images.

Image & Mask Size Analysis

📏 Detailed Size Metrics

Class Image Dimensions (W x H) Avg Prostate Size Avg Prostate Ratio Avg Target1 Size Avg Target1 Ratio Avg Target2 Size Avg Target2 Ratio
class1 256x256 2,624 px 4.00% 912 px 1.39% 235 px 0.36%
class2 256x256, 512x512 2,365 px 3.61% 169 px 0.22% 110 px 0.15%
class3 448x448, 256x256, 512x512 1,458 px 2.23% 187 px 0.22% 199 px 0.25%
class4 256x256, 512x512 2,327 px 3.55% 476 px 0.73% 231 px 0.35%
💡 Size Insights: The mask sizes and ratios provide important context for understanding the segmentation task difficulty. Smaller masks (lower ratios) are typically more challenging to segment accurately. Prostate masks generally cover 5-15% of the image, while target lesions are much smaller at 0.5-3%.

Case-Level Analysis

The case-level analysis reveals important patterns in data distribution and mask availability across different cases. Understanding these patterns is crucial for proper train/validation/test splits and model training strategies.

Detailed Statistics

Class Images Cases Series Prostate Target1 Target2 Total Masks
class1 1,024 17 17 566 119 34 719
class2 3,446 58 58 1,852 322 86 2,260
class3 3,489 60 60 1,781 345 58 2,184
class4 3,403 59 59 1,762 720 33 2,515
TOTAL 11,362 194 194 5,961 1,506 211 7,678

Recommendations

✅ Training Strategy

  • Multi-class Training: Use all three mask types (prostate, target1, target2) for comprehensive segmentation
  • Class Balancing: Consider weighted sampling or data augmentation for underrepresented classes
  • 2.5D Approach: Leverage the stack depth of 5 slices for 3D context while maintaining computational efficiency
  • Train/Val Split: Use case-level splitting to prevent data leakage between train and validation sets

⚠️ Data Considerations

  • Mask Coverage: Not all images have corresponding masks - use skip_no_masks=True during training
  • Target Masks: Target1 and Target2 masks are less abundant than Prostate masks
  • Variable Slices: Cases have varying numbers of slices - ensure proper handling in data pipeline

📊 Next Steps

  1. Review mask quality using validation visualizations
  2. Set up train/validation/test splits at the case level
  3. Configure data augmentation pipeline for balanced training
  4. Start with single-class (prostate) training before multi-class
  5. Monitor class-specific metrics during training
